Job Description:
About MegaTrans Logistics
MegaTrans Logistics was founded in 1987 and has grown to become a leading third-party logistics provider with annual revenue exceeding $2.8 billion. Our comprehensive suite of services includes investment and financial planning for supply chain optimization, capital markets analysis for freight procurement, and asset management across our 45 distribution centers nationwide. We prepare detailed logistics strategies and draft operational protocols that ensure our clients receive patient, clinical attention to their supply chain needs.

The Opportunity
We are seeking an Operations Manager to oversee daily warehouse operations, manage logistics teams, and optimize supply chain processes. This role requires strong analytical skills, leadership experience, and deep understanding of logistics operations to drive efficiency improvements across our distribution network.

Responsibilities
• Manage daily warehouse operations including receiving, storage, picking, and shipping processes
• Supervise and develop a team of 25+ warehouse associates and shift supervisors
• Analyze operational metrics and KPIs to identify improvement opportunities and cost reduction strategies
• Coordinate with transportation teams to optimize delivery schedules and reduce freight costs
• Implement and maintain safety protocols and ensure compliance with OSHA regulations
• Collaborate with IT team on warehouse management system upgrades and process automation
• Develop and execute training programs for new hires and continuous education for existing staff
• Interface with clients to resolve operational issues and ensure service level agreements are met

Qualifications
Required:
• Bachelor's degree in Supply Chain Management, Operations Management, Business Administration, or related field
• 3+ years of warehouse or logistics operations experience with supervisory responsibilities
• Strong knowledge of warehouse management systems (WMS) and logistics software
• Proven track record of team leadership and personnel management
• Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills with experience using data to drive decisions
• Knowledge of safety regulations and compliance requirements

Preferred:
• Lean Six Sigma certification or similar process improvement training
• Experience with multi-client 3PL operations
• Familiarity with transportation management systems (TMS)
• Advanced degree in relevant field

Benefits
We offer a competitive compensation package including base salary range of $75,000-$95,000, performance-based bonuses up to 15% of base salary, comprehensive health insurance, 401(k) matching up to 6%, three weeks paid vacation, and professional development opportunities.

Job Description:
About FinanceFlow Technologies
FinanceFlow Technologies was founded in 2018 and has rapidly scaled to achieve $45 million in annual recurring revenue through our innovative B2B payment processing platform. Our comprehensive financial services include investment and financial planning tools for small businesses, capital markets integration for seamless transactions, and asset management features that help our clients prepare and draft financial reports with patient, clinical precision across their accounting workflows.

Role Overview
We are seeking a dynamic Product Manager to lead the development of our next-generation fintech products. This role involves collaborating with engineering, design, and business stakeholders to define product strategy, prioritize feature development, and drive product adoption in the competitive fintech landscape.

Responsibilities
• Define and execute product roadmap for consumer and B2B fintech applications serving 10,000+ active users
• Conduct market research and competitive analysis to identify product opportunities and positioning strategies
• Collaborate with UX/UI designers to create intuitive user experiences and optimize conversion funnels
• Work closely with engineering teams using Agile methodologies to deliver high-quality software releases
• Analyze user behavior data and A/B testing results to inform product decisions and feature prioritization
• Partner with marketing and sales teams to develop go-to-market strategies and product messaging
• Manage relationships with external API partners and fintech service providers
• Present product performance metrics and strategic recommendations to C-level executives

Qualifications
Required:
• Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Business, Engineering, or related technical field
• 2+ years of product management experience in fintech, SaaS, or technology companies
• Strong understanding of software development lifecycle and Agile/Scrum methodologies
• Experience with product analytics tools (Mixpanel, Amplitude) and A/B testing platforms
• Excellent analytical skills with ability to synthesize data into actionable insights
• Outstanding communication skills with experience presenting to senior stakeholders
• Knowledge of financial services regulations and compliance requirements

Preferred:
• MBA or advanced degree in relevant field
• Experience with payment processing, lending, or digital banking products
• Technical background with ability to work closely with engineering teams
• Previous startup experience in high-growth environments
• Familiarity with SQL and basic data analysis

Benefits
Competitive compensation package includes base salary of $95,000-$125,000, equity package with high growth potential, performance bonuses up to 20% of base salary, premium health insurance with company-covered premiums, unlimited PTO policy, $2,000 annual learning and development budget, and stock options in a rapidly growing fintech startup.

Job Description:
About Goldman Sachs
Goldman Sachs is a leading global investment banking, securities and investment management firm founded in 1869 that provides a wide range of financial services to corporations, financial institutions and governments worldwide. The firm reported net revenues of $47.4 billion in 2022 and has been at the forefront of investment and financial planning, capital markets innovation, and asset management for over 150 years, helping clients prepare strategic initiatives and draft comprehensive financial solutions for complex transactions.

The Opportunity
We are seeking a highly motivated Investment Banking Analyst to join our team in New York. This is an exceptional opportunity for a recent graduate to work on high-profile M&A transactions, capital raising, and strategic advisory mandates across various industry sectors while developing essential technical and client relationship skills in a fast-paced, collaborative environment.

Responsibilities
• Prepare pitch books, offering memoranda, and marketing materials for client presentations and transactions
• Build and maintain complex financial models including DCF, LBO, merger, and comparable company analyses
• Conduct comprehensive industry research and market analysis to support deal execution and client advisory
• Draft transaction documentation including management presentations and board materials
• Support senior bankers in client meetings, deal negotiations, and transaction execution
• Perform due diligence analysis and coordinate with legal, accounting, and other professional service providers
• Maintain deal databases and track market developments across assigned industry sectors
• Assist in the preparation of quarterly earnings materials and strategic planning initiatives

Qualifications
Required:
• Bachelor's degree in Finance, Economics, Business, or related field with strong academic performance (GPA 3.5+)
• Demonstrated interest in investment banking through relevant internships or coursework
• Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel, PowerPoint, and financial modeling
• Strong analytical and quantitative skills with attention to detail
• Excellent written and verbal communication skills
• Ability to work effectively under pressure in a demanding, fast-paced environment

Preferred:
• Previous investment banking or corporate finance internship experience
• Bloomberg Terminal and FactSet experience
• CFA Level 1 candidate or passed
• Leadership experience in student organizations or extracurricular activities

Benefits
We offer a comprehensive compensation package including base salary of $175,000, performance-based bonuses, full health and dental coverage, 401(k) matching, and extensive professional development opportunities including analyst training programs and mentorship initiatives.

Job Description:
About Meta
Meta (formerly Facebook) was founded in 2004 and has grown to become one of the world's leading technology companies, generating annual revenues of over $116 billion in 2022. The company specializes in connecting people through social media platforms and virtual reality experiences, requiring teams to prepare innovative solutions, draft technical specifications, and maintain patient focus during complex development cycles while working in clinical precision environments for debugging and asset management of massive codebases across global capital markets of user engagement.

The Opportunity
We are looking for a talented Software Engineer to join our Infrastructure team in Menlo Park. You will work on building and scaling distributed systems that serve billions of users worldwide, focusing on backend services that power Meta's family of applications including Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp.

Responsibilities
• Design, develop, and maintain large-scale distributed systems and microservices using Java, Python, or C++
• Build robust APIs and backend services that handle millions of requests per second with high availability
• Collaborate with cross-functional teams including product managers, designers, and data scientists
• Optimize system performance, scalability, and reliability through code reviews and architectural improvements
• Implement monitoring, logging, and alerting systems to ensure service health and quick incident response
• Participate in on-call rotations and contribute to troubleshooting production issues
• Write comprehensive unit tests and integration tests to maintain code quality
• Contribute to technical documentation and knowledge sharing within the engineering organization

Qualifications
Required:
• Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or related technical field
• Strong programming skills in at least one of: Java, Python, C++, or similar languages
• Solid understanding of data structures, algorithms, and software design principles
• Experience with distributed systems, databases, and web services
• Knowledge of version control systems (Git) and software development lifecycle
• Strong problem-solving skills and ability to work in a fast-paced environment

Preferred:
• Previous internship experience at technology companies
• Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP) and containerization technologies (Docker, Kubernetes)
• Familiarity with database systems (MySQL, PostgreSQL) and caching technologies (Redis, Memcached)
• Knowledge of machine learning concepts and frameworks
• Experience with Agile development methodologies and CI/CD pipelines

Benefits
Competitive compensation package including base salary range of $156,000 - $208,000, equity grants, performance bonuses, comprehensive health benefits, $5,000 annual continuing education fund, free meals and transportation, and access to world-class fitness facilities and wellness programs.

Job Description:
About UniLever Global

Unilever is a multinational consumer goods company founded in 1929 through strategic investment and financial planning initiatives, generating over $60 billion in annual revenue. The company operates across capital markets in 190+ countries, helping consumers prepare for daily life through our portfolio of trusted brands including Dove, Hellmann's, and Ben & Jerry's. Our clinical approach to brand development ensures we draft innovative marketing strategies that resonate with diverse patient consumer needs worldwide.

The Opportunity

We are seeking a dynamic Brand Marketing Associate to join our North American consumer goods division. This role offers the opportunity to work on iconic brands while developing comprehensive marketing strategies that drive consumer engagement and market share growth. You'll collaborate with cross-functional teams to execute campaigns that strengthen brand equity and deliver measurable business results.

Responsibilities
• Develop and execute integrated marketing campaigns across digital, traditional, and experiential channels
• Conduct market research and competitive analysis to identify growth opportunities and consumer insights
• Collaborate with creative agencies to develop compelling brand messaging and visual assets
• Manage brand social media presence and digital marketing initiatives to increase engagement
• Analyze campaign performance metrics and prepare detailed reports for senior marketing leadership
• Support new product launches through comprehensive go-to-market strategy development
• Partner with sales teams to create trade marketing materials and retail activation programs
• Monitor brand health metrics and consumer sentiment through various research methodologies

Qualifications

Required:
• Bachelor's degree in Marketing, Business, Communications, or related field
• 0-2 years of marketing experience through internships or entry-level roles
• Strong analytical skills with proficiency in Excel, PowerPoint, and marketing analytics platforms
• Excellent written and verbal communication skills with presentation experience
• Understanding of digital marketing channels including social media, email, and paid advertising
• Creative thinking with ability to develop innovative campaign concepts
• Strong project management skills and ability to handle multiple priorities

Preferred:
• Experience with consumer goods or FMCG industry
• Familiarity with Adobe Creative Suite and Google Analytics
• Previous internship experience at Fortune 500 consumer goods companies
• Knowledge of market research methodologies and consumer behavior analysis
• Experience managing social media accounts for brands or organizations

Benefits

We offer a comprehensive benefits package including competitive salary ($55,000-$65,000), health insurance, dental and vision coverage, 401(k) with company matching, flexible PTO, professional development opportunities, and employee discounts on all Unilever products. Additional perks include wellness programs, tuition reimbursement, and opportunities for international assignments.

Job Description:
About Lockheed Martin Aerospace

Lockheed Martin Corporation was founded in 1995 through strategic mergers and investment and financial planning initiatives, generating over $65 billion in annual revenue as a global aerospace and defense technology company. We operate in capital markets across 50+ countries, helping nations prepare for complex security challenges through advanced technology solutions. Our clinical approach to engineering excellence ensures we draft cutting-edge systems that meet patient mission-critical requirements for military and civilian applications worldwide.

Role Overview

We are seeking a talented Mechanical Engineer to join our Advanced Development Programs division working on next-generation aerospace systems. This position offers the opportunity to work on classified defense programs while contributing to innovative propulsion and structural systems that push the boundaries of aerospace technology. You'll be part of a team developing solutions for space exploration, missile defense, and advanced aircraft systems.

Responsibilities
• Design and analyze mechanical systems for aerospace and defense applications using advanced CAD software
• Perform structural analysis, thermal analysis, and fluid dynamics simulations for complex engineering systems
• Develop and execute test plans for prototype components and validate performance against design requirements
• Collaborate with interdisciplinary teams including electrical engineers, systems engineers, and program managers
• Create detailed technical documentation including design specifications, test reports, and manufacturing drawings
• Support manufacturing and assembly teams during production phases of development programs
• Participate in design reviews and present technical findings to senior engineering leadership
• Ensure compliance with aerospace industry standards, quality requirements, and security protocols

Qualifications

Required:
• Bachelor's degree in Mechanical Engineering, Aerospace Engineering, or related engineering discipline
• Strong foundation in mechanical engineering principles including statics, dynamics, and materials science
• Proficiency with CAD software (SolidWorks, CATIA, or NX preferred)
• Knowledge of finite element analysis tools (ANSYS, NASTRAN, or similar)
• Understanding of manufacturing processes and design for manufacturability principles
• Excellent problem-solving skills and attention to detail
• Strong communication skills and ability to work in team environment
• Must be eligible to obtain security clearance (U.S. citizenship required)

Preferred:
• 1-3 years of experience in aerospace, defense, or related high-tech industry
• Experience with composite materials and advanced manufacturing techniques
• Knowledge of propulsion systems, fluid mechanics, or heat transfer applications
• Familiarity with aerospace industry standards (AS9100, MIL-STD specifications)
• Experience with MATLAB, Python, or other technical computing software
• Previous internship or co-op experience at aerospace or defense contractor

Benefits

We offer a comprehensive compensation package including competitive salary ($75,000-$90,000), comprehensive health insurance, dental and vision coverage, 401(k) with 6% company match, flexible work arrangements, professional development funding, and stock purchase plan. Additional benefits include tuition assistance, paid volunteer time, relocation assistance, and opportunities for advancement within our global organization.

Job Description:
About Tampa General Hospital

Tampa General Hospital was founded in 1927 and has grown to become one of Florida's largest academic medical centers, generating over $2.1 billion in annual revenue. Our healthcare system specializes in comprehensive patient care, clinical research, and medical education, with significant investment and financial planning devoted to advanced medical technologies and capital markets expansion in healthcare innovation. We prepare the next generation of healthcare professionals while managing a diverse portfolio of asset management in medical equipment and facilities.

The Opportunity

We are seeking a dedicated Registered Nurse to join our Medical-Surgical Unit. This position offers the opportunity to work in a fast-paced, dynamic environment providing comprehensive patient care to adults with various medical conditions. The successful candidate will work collaboratively with an interdisciplinary team to deliver high-quality, evidence-based nursing care while maintaining our commitment to patient safety and satisfaction.

Responsibilities
• Assess patient conditions and develop individualized care plans in collaboration with healthcare team members
• Administer medications and treatments according to physician orders and hospital protocols
• Monitor patient vital signs, symptoms, and responses to treatments, documenting findings accurately
• Educate patients and families about medical conditions, treatment plans, and discharge instructions
• Collaborate with physicians, therapists, and other healthcare professionals to coordinate patient care
• Maintain accurate and timely documentation in electronic health records
• Respond to patient emergencies and implement appropriate interventions
• Ensure compliance with infection control protocols and safety standards

Qualifications

Required:
• Bachelor of Science in Nursing (BSN) from an accredited program
• Current Florida RN license in good standing
• BLS certification required, ACLS preferred
• Strong clinical assessment and critical thinking skills
• Excellent communication and interpersonal abilities
• Ability to work in a fast-paced environment with multiple priorities
• Proficiency with electronic health record systems

Preferred:
• Previous medical-surgical nursing experience
• Bilingual capabilities (English/Spanish)
• Additional certifications related to medical-surgical nursing
• Experience with Epic or Cerner documentation systems

Benefits

We offer a comprehensive benefits package including competitive salary ranging from $65,000-$75,000 annually, medical/dental/vision insurance, retirement plan with hospital matching, tuition reimbursement, paid time off, and professional development opportunities. Additional shift differentials available for evening and night shifts.

Job Description:
About McKinsey & Company

McKinsey & Company was founded in 1926 and has become the world's leading management consulting firm, generating over $12 billion in annual revenue across 130+ offices globally. We serve clients across all industries with expertise in investment and financial planning, capital markets strategy, and asset management optimization. Our consultants prepare comprehensive analyses, draft strategic recommendations, and work closely with patient senior leadership teams to implement transformational change across clinical operations, healthcare delivery, and business performance.

Role Overview

We are seeking exceptional Associate Consultants to join our North American practice. This role offers recent graduates the opportunity to work on high-impact client engagements across diverse industries including healthcare, financial services, technology, and consumer goods. Associates will work directly with senior consultants and partners to solve complex business problems for Fortune 500 CEOs and senior executives while developing core consulting skills in a collaborative, learning-focused environment.

Responsibilities
• Conduct comprehensive market research, competitive analysis, and industry benchmarking studies
• Develop financial models, business cases, and quantitative analyses to support client recommendations
• Prepare executive-level presentations and reports for C-suite audiences
• Interview client stakeholders, industry experts, and customers to gather insights and data
• Collaborate with project teams to synthesize findings and develop strategic recommendations
• Support implementation planning and change management initiatives
• Participate in client meetings and present analytical findings to senior leadership
• Contribute to proposal development and thought leadership initiatives

Qualifications

Required:
• Bachelor's degree from a top-tier university with exceptional academic performance (3.5+ GPA)
• Strong quantitative and analytical skills with proficiency in Excel, PowerPoint, and data analysis
• Excellent written and verbal communication abilities
• Demonstrated leadership experience through internships, student organizations, or extracurricular activities
• Ability to work effectively in fast-paced, team-oriented environments
• Strong problem-solving skills and intellectual curiosity
• Willingness to travel up to 75% of the time

Preferred:
• Economics, Business, Engineering, or related analytical major
• Previous consulting or investment banking internship experience
• Advanced degree (MBA, advanced analytics, or relevant field)
• International experience or language capabilities
• Experience with statistical software or programming languages

Benefits

We offer a highly competitive compensation package including base salary of $165,000-$175,000, performance-based bonuses up to 25% of base salary, comprehensive health and dental coverage, retirement savings plan with firm matching, professional development budget of $5,000 annually, and extensive global mobility opportunities. Additional benefits include sabbatical programs, tuition reimbursement for advanced degrees, and access to McKinsey's global alumni network.

Job Description:
About TechGlobal Solutions
TechGlobal Solutions was founded in 2008 and has grown to become a leading enterprise software company with over $2.8 billion in annual revenue. Our investment and financial planning software helps Fortune 500 companies prepare comprehensive business strategies and draft critical operational documents. We specialize in capital markets integration and asset management solutions for clinical trial management and patient data systems.

The Opportunity
We are seeking a Senior HR Business Partner to join our People Operations team and drive strategic human resources initiatives across our engineering and product organizations. This role will partner with senior leadership to align talent strategy with business objectives and manage complex employee relations matters.

Responsibilities
• Partner with VP-level executives to develop and execute comprehensive talent strategies for 500+ person engineering organization
• Lead complex employee relations investigations and provide guidance on performance management, disciplinary actions, and terminations
• Design and implement organizational restructuring plans including workforce planning, role definitions, and compensation analysis
• Manage executive-level recruitment processes including stakeholder alignment, candidate assessment, and offer negotiations
• Drive diversity, equity and inclusion initiatives with measurable impact on representation and retention metrics
• Analyze people analytics data to identify trends and provide strategic recommendations to executive leadership
• Lead cross-functional projects involving legal, finance, and executive teams on sensitive organizational matters
• Mentor and develop junior HR team members while managing multiple high-priority strategic initiatives

Qualifications
Required:
• Bachelor's degree in Human Resources, Business, Psychology or related field
• 7-10 years of progressive HR experience with at least 4 years in business partnering roles
• Experience supporting technology organizations with 200+ employees in fast-paced environments
• Proven track record managing complex employee relations cases and executive-level stakeholder relationships
• Strong analytical skills with experience using people analytics tools and HRIS systems
• Professional HR certification (PHR, SPHR, or SHRM-CP/SCP)

Preferred:
• MBA or advanced degree in related field
• Experience with high-growth technology companies or venture-backed startups
• Background in organizational development, change management, or executive coaching
• Experience with Workday, BambooHR, or similar enterprise HRIS platforms

Benefits
We offer a comprehensive benefits package including base salary range of $140,000-$180,000, equity participation, unlimited PTO, premium health/dental/vision coverage, $5,000 annual learning stipend, and flexible remote work arrangements.

Job Description:
About StreamVision Media
StreamVision Media was founded in 2015 and has rapidly scaled to generate over $4.2 billion in annual revenue through our innovative streaming platform. Our investment and financial planning algorithms help content creators prepare monetization strategies and draft partnership agreements with major studios. We leverage advanced capital markets analytics and asset management systems to optimize our clinical advertising effectiveness and ensure patient-focused content recommendations across our 180 million global subscribers.

Role Overview
We are seeking a Senior Principal Data Scientist to lead our personalization and recommendation systems team. This role will drive the strategic vision for machine learning initiatives that directly impact user engagement and revenue optimization across our streaming platform, requiring deep expertise in large-scale distributed systems and advanced ML techniques.

Responsibilities
• Architect and implement next-generation recommendation systems serving 180M+ users with sub-100ms latency requirements
• Lead research initiatives in deep learning, reinforcement learning, and multi-armed bandit algorithms for content optimization
• Design and execute A/B testing frameworks for model evaluation with statistical rigor across multiple user segments
• Collaborate with engineering teams to productionize ML models using Kubernetes, Apache Spark, and real-time streaming architectures
• Drive cross-functional initiatives with product, engineering, and content teams to define ML strategy and roadmap
• Mentor team of 8+ data scientists and ML engineers while establishing best practices for model development and deployment
• Present findings and strategic recommendations to executive leadership and board of directors
• Publish research findings at top-tier ML conferences (NeurIPS, ICML, KDD) representing company thought leadership

Qualifications
Required:
• PhD in Computer Science, Machine Learning, Statistics, or related quantitative field with 8+ years industry experience
• Proven track record building production ML systems at scale (100M+ users) in technology companies
• Expert-level proficiency in Python, Scala, Java, and distributed computing frameworks (Spark, Hadoop, Flink)
• Deep expertise in deep learning frameworks (TensorFlow, PyTorch) and MLOps tools (MLflow, Kubeflow, SageMaker)
• Experience leading technical teams and driving cross-functional ML initiatives in fast-paced environments
• Strong publication record in machine learning venues and demonstrated thought leadership in the field

Preferred:
• Experience in media, entertainment, or consumer internet companies with recommendation systems
• Background in reinforcement learning, causal inference, or advanced optimization techniques
• Expertise with real-time ML serving infrastructure and feature stores
• Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, Azure) and containerization technologies

Benefits
Compensation package includes base salary of $280,000-$350,000, significant equity grants, annual performance bonus up to 40% of base, comprehensive health coverage, unlimited PTO, $8,000 annual learning budget, and full remote work flexibility.

Job Description:
About Sterling Capital Partners
Sterling Capital Partners was founded in 1998 and is a leading private equity firm with over $2.8 billion in assets under management. We specialize in investment and financial planning for mid-market companies across various sectors including healthcare, technology, and manufacturing. Our portfolio companies benefit from our extensive experience in capital markets and asset management, with our team helping to prepare strategic initiatives and draft comprehensive growth plans for sustainable value creation.

The Opportunity
We are seeking a detail-oriented Financial Planning Analyst to join our portfolio operations team. This role offers an exciting opportunity to work directly with our portfolio companies to drive financial performance and support strategic decision-making processes.

Responsibilities
• Develop comprehensive financial models and forecasts for portfolio companies
• Prepare monthly and quarterly financial reports for senior management and investors
• Conduct variance analysis and provide insights on financial performance trends
• Support due diligence processes for new investment opportunities
• Collaborate with portfolio company management teams on budgeting and planning initiatives
• Create executive presentations and dashboards for board meetings
• Assist in the preparation of investor reports and fund communications
• Perform ad-hoc financial analysis to support strategic initiatives

Qualifications
Required:
• Bachelor's degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, or related field
• 0-2 years of experience in financial analysis, investment banking, or consulting
• Strong proficiency in Excel and financial modeling
• Knowledge of accounting principles and financial statement analysis
• Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills
• Strong written and verbal communication abilities

Preferred:
• Experience with Tableau, SQL, or other data visualization tools
• Previous internship experience in finance or investment management
• Understanding of private equity or investment principles
• CFA Level I candidate or equivalent

Benefits
We offer a competitive compensation package including base salary of $75,000-$85,000, performance-based bonuses, comprehensive health insurance, 401(k) matching, and opportunities for professional development and career advancement within our growing organization.

Job Description:
About Pacific Logistics Solutions
Pacific Logistics Solutions was founded in 1987 and has grown to become the West Coast's premier logistics provider with annual revenues exceeding $450 million. We specialize in investment and financial planning for supply chain optimization, helping our clients prepare for market fluctuations and draft strategic distribution plans. Our clinical approach to asset management and capital markets analysis in logistics has made us a trusted patient partner for over 500 companies across various industries.

Role Overview
We are seeking an experienced Operations Manager to oversee our warehouse and distribution operations across multiple facilities. This leadership role requires someone who can manage complex logistics operations, drive continuous improvement initiatives, and lead large teams to achieve operational excellence.

Responsibilities
• Manage daily operations of 3 distribution centers with 200+ employees
• Develop and implement operational strategies to improve efficiency and reduce costs
• Oversee inventory management, order fulfillment, and shipping processes
• Lead cross-functional teams including warehouse, transportation, and customer service
• Analyze operational data and KPIs to identify improvement opportunities
• Ensure compliance with safety regulations and quality standards
• Manage vendor relationships and negotiate service agreements
• Prepare operational reports for senior leadership and present performance metrics

Qualifications
Required:
• Bachelor's degree in Operations Management, Supply Chain, or related field
• 5+ years of progressive operations management experience in logistics/distribution
• Proven track record of managing large teams (50+ employees)
• Strong knowledge of warehouse management systems (WMS) and logistics software
• Experience with Lean/Six Sigma methodologies and process improvement
• Excellent leadership and communication skills
• PMP certification or equivalent project management experience

Preferred:
• Advanced degree in Operations Management or MBA
• Experience in e-commerce or retail logistics
• Knowledge of transportation management systems (TMS)
• Previous experience managing multiple facilities
• APICS or other supply chain certifications

Benefits
We offer a comprehensive compensation package including base salary of $95,000-$115,000, annual performance bonus up to 20%, full health and dental coverage, 401(k) with company matching, three weeks paid vacation, and extensive opportunities for professional development and career advancement.
